title: Build and Ship a Safe Evaluated AI Agent with Python 3.14 and Gemini 3.5
description: Python, HTTP, prompt, and agent-loop learners who need to design, implement, evaluate, secure, operate, and defend a tool-using Gemini agent instead of copy a brittle while-loop demo or hide core contracts behind a framework. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; product and risk contracts; reproducible repositories; gemini interactions api; gemini 3.5 capability and stability selection; google-genai 2.11.0; deterministic provider ports and fakes; typed interaction parts and state; pydantic 2.13.4 and json schema 2020-12 structured output; typed tool schemas, allowlisted dispatch, least agency, and complete function-call circulation; explicit bounded loops; asyncio taskgroup concurrency; human approval and durable resume; session events, checkpoints, replay, context compaction, and poisoning-resistant memory; retry and idempotency; accessible event streaming; owasp-informed injection, exfiltration, memory, tool, identity, code-execution, multi-agent, and denial-of-wallet defense; provider data terms, privacy, retention, and sensitive-use review; deterministic, trajectory, rubric, model-judge, and human-calibrated evaluation; opentelemetry genai observability; token, cost, rate, caching, latency, capacity, and overload control; google-adk 2.4.0 translation and conformance; contained delegation; sandboxed code and computer-use boundaries; packaging, api, deployment, migration, canary, rollback, kill switch, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit provider, model, sdk, http, identity, secret, storage, sandbox, browser, database, telemetry, accessibility, privacy, security, load, cost, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic agent deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-agent defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves stakeholder task, risk tier, authority, revision, Python, SDK, API and model identity, interaction and call IDs, state and budget, tool policy, approval, validation, accessibility, privacy and security boundary, changed-case test, failure, recovery, and explicit browser-versus-controlled-system limits before adding one agent capability.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; those require controlled evidence.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate interaction, call, result, state, approval, evaluation, trace, or recovery evidence, exact observable output, a changed and rejected case, a test that detects a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder evidence, and a named owner for remaining risk.

competencies.statement: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model.
misconceptions: A fluent demo is sufficient evidence of stakeholder value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model.

competencies.statement: Compare deterministic code, workflow, model-assisted step, and agent loop and choose the least variable architecture that satisfies the task.
misconceptions: Every multi-step language task benefits from an autonomous loop.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare deterministic code, workflow, model-assisted step, and agent loop and choose the least variable architecture that satisfies the task.

competencies.statement: Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.
misconceptions: Risk depends only on whether the model can generate harmful words.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.

competencies.statement: Specify task, safety, tool, accessibility, latency, cost, refusal, recovery, and user-outcome evidence with acceptance thresholds.
misconceptions: One average response-quality score captures agent success.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify task, safety, tool, accessibility, latency, cost, refusal, recovery, and user-outcome evidence with acceptance thresholds.

competencies.statement: Define prohibited tasks, uncertainty disclosures, stop conditions, escalation owners, support paths, and authority revocation.
misconceptions: The agent should always attempt the task rather than refuse or escalate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define prohibited tasks, uncertainty disclosures, stop conditions, escalation owners, support paths, and authority revocation.

competencies.statement: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, fixtures, secrets, licenses, threat model, and recovery anchor before editing.
misconceptions: A prototype agent is too small to need repository and rollback evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, fixtures, secrets, licenses, threat model, and recovery anchor before editing.

competencies.statement: Record Python implementation and version, platform, event-loop behavior, SSL backend, free-threaded status, encoding environment, and supported matrix.
misconceptions: Any Python 3 runtime gives identical async, SDK, validation, and packaging behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record Python implementation and version, platform, event-loop behavior, SSL backend, free-threaded status, encoding environment, and supported matrix.

competencies.statement: Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify package sources and hashes, review licenses and advisories, and prove isolated installation.
misconceptions: Broad minimum versions make an agent installation reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify package sources and hashes, review licenses and advisories, and prove isolated installation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish google-genai, deprecated predecessor SDKs, google-adk, API versions, model versions, stable features, previews, and migrations.
misconceptions: All Google generative AI Python packages expose the same current API surface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish google-genai, deprecated predecessor SDKs, google-adk, API versions, model versions, stable features, previews, and migrations.

competencies.statement: Rebuild the repository from a clean environment and reconcile versions, import behavior, fixed fixtures, tests, artifacts, and shutdown state.
misconceptions: A working developer virtual environment proves the declared dependency set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rebuild the repository from a clean environment and reconcile versions, import behavior, fixed fixtures, tests, artifacts, and shutdown state.

competencies.statement: Use the Gemini Interactions API for new agent work and document when a legacy generateContent integration must remain during migration.
misconceptions: Every Gemini endpoint and SDK example represents the same recommended state model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use the Gemini Interactions API for new agent work and document when a legacy generateContent integration must remain during migration.

competencies.statement: Discover and record model support for interactions, function calls, structured output, multimodal parts, thinking, streaming, context, and limits.
misconceptions: A model family name guarantees every capability and limit across versions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Discover and record model support for interactions, function calls, structured output, multimodal parts, thinking, streaming, context, and limits.

competencies.statement: Separate stable, preview, experimental, deprecated, and retired features and assign migration, fallback, and re-review owners.
misconceptions: A documented preview feature is production-stable by definition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate stable, preview, experimental, deprecated, and retired features and assign migration, fallback, and re-review owners.

competencies.statement: Choose system instruction, temperature, thinking level, output limits, safety configuration, and tools from task evidence and re-declare per interaction.
misconceptions: One creative generation configuration is appropriate for every agent step.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose system instruction, temperature, thinking level, output limits, safety configuration, and tools from task evidence and re-declare per interaction.

competencies.statement: Define a narrow typed provider interface that preserves provider-specific steps and usage while keeping orchestration testable and replaceable.
misconceptions: Provider portability means discarding provider-specific state and metadata.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a narrow typed provider interface that preserves provider-specific steps and usage while keeping orchestration testable and replaceable.

competencies.statement: Express model requests, typed steps, usage, errors, cancellation, and async lifecycle through a minimal Python protocol.
misconceptions: Mocking the entire SDK object graph is a stable provider abstraction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express model requests, typed steps, usage, errors, cancellation, and async lifecycle through a minimal Python protocol.

competencies.statement: Build a scripted fake that consumes expected requests and emits deterministic model output, tool calls, safety blocks, usage, and faults.
misconceptions: Returning one canned string tests an agent loop adequately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build a scripted fake that consumes expected requests and emits deterministic model output, tool calls, safety blocks, usage, and faults.

competencies.statement: Store sanitized versioned provider fixtures with schema, model, API, SDK, timestamp, purpose, and explicit replay limits.
misconceptions: A captured response remains valid forever without its request or version identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store sanitized versioned provider fixtures with schema, model, API, SDK, timestamp, purpose, and explicit replay limits.

competencies.statement: Hash canonical request inputs and correlate every response, step, call, result, error, and usage record to one test case.
misconceptions: Test names alone provide sufficient trajectory identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Hash canonical request inputs and correlate every response, step, call, result, error, and usage record to one test case.

competencies.statement: Keep unit and routine integration tests offline and reserve authorized live-provider smoke, compatibility, and quality runs for controlled gates.
misconceptions: Live model calls make a test suite more realistic and therefore more reliable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep unit and routine integration tests offline and reserve authorized live-provider smoke, compatibility, and quality runs for controlled gates.

competencies.statement: Model each interaction with stable local identity, provider interaction ID, parent, state mode, timestamps, model, configuration, and outcome.
misconceptions: A conversation is only an ordered list of plain role and text pairs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model each interaction with stable local identity, provider interaction ID, parent, state mode, timestamps, model, configuration, and outcome.

competencies.statement: Preserve text, function call, function result, image, file, refusal, safety, and model-output parts without lossy string flattening.
misconceptions: All multimodal and tool content can be safely converted to one text field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve text, function call, function result, image, file, refusal, safety, and model-output parts without lossy string flattening.

competencies.statement: Use previous_interaction_id deliberately, distinguish server-side history from stateless replay, and preserve local audit evidence.
misconceptions: Passing a prior interaction ID preserves every parameter and tool automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use previous_interaction_id deliberately, distinguish server-side history from stateless replay, and preserve local audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Re-declare interaction-scoped tools, system instruction, and generation configuration on every turn that needs them.
misconceptions: Provider-side conversation state implicitly carries all prior request configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Re-declare interaction-scoped tools, system instruction, and generation configuration on every turn that needs them.

competencies.statement: Choose stateless, provider-stateful, or application-stateful operation from privacy, deletion, audit, latency, cost, and recovery requirements.
misconceptions: Server-side state is always cheaper and safer than application-managed state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose stateless, provider-stateful, or application-stateful operation from privacy, deletion, audit, latency, cost, and recovery requirements.

competencies.statement: Use structured output for final typed data and function calling for intermediate actions rather than confusing the two contracts.
misconceptions: Structured output and function calling are interchangeable ways to invoke application effects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use structured output for final typed data and function calling for intermediate actions rather than confusing the two contracts.

competencies.statement: Validate provider output with strict Pydantic models, explicit bounds, discriminated variants, forbidden extras, and actionable errors.
misconceptions: Pydantic coercion makes any model-produced value safe for downstream use.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate provider output with strict Pydantic models, explicit bounds, discriminated variants, forbidden extras, and actionable errors.

competencies.statement: Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.
misconceptions: A valid Draft 2020-12 schema is necessarily supported by every model endpoint.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.

competencies.statement: Represent safety blocks, refusals, truncation, missing output, invalid schema, and transport errors as distinct non-success outcomes.
misconceptions: No parseable object means the same failure regardless of provider finish state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent safety blocks, refusals, truncation, missing output, invalid schema, and transport errors as distinct non-success outcomes.

competencies.statement: Bound repair attempts, preserve original invalid evidence, avoid semantic invention, and reject or escalate when validity cannot be proven.
misconceptions: Repeatedly asking the model to fix JSON guarantees the intended meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound repair attempts, preserve original invalid evidence, avoid semantic invention, and reject or escalate when validity cannot be proven.

competencies.statement: Define one narrow tool purpose, effect class, caller, user benefit, authority, timeout, idempotency, and prohibited use.
misconceptions: A generic execute command tool is more flexible and therefore better designed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define one narrow tool purpose, effect class, caller, user benefit, authority, timeout, idempotency, and prohibited use.

competencies.statement: Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.
misconceptions: Parameter names and Python annotations eliminate the need for explicit constraints.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.

competencies.statement: Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.
misconceptions: Long marketing prose helps the model choose tools accurately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.

competencies.statement: Replace ambiguous strings with domain types, enums, identifiers, units, timestamps, and validated references without leaking secrets.
misconceptions: Every identifier, amount, and timestamp is safely represented as an unconstrained string.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Replace ambiguous strings with domain types, enums, identifiers, units, timestamps, and validated references without leaking secrets.

competencies.statement: Version tool contracts and provide compatibility, migration, deprecation, and replay tests for changed parameters and results.
misconceptions: Tool schemas can change in place because the model sees only the latest definition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version tool contracts and provide compatibility, migration, deprecation, and replay tests for changed parameters and results.

competencies.statement: Register immutable tool name, schema version, handler, effect class, policy, timeout, owner, and result contract in one auditable catalog.
misconceptions: Looking up any callable by model-provided name is a valid registry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Register immutable tool name, schema version, handler, effect class, policy, timeout, owner, and result contract in one auditable catalog.

competencies.statement: Preserve provider call ID, local attempt ID, interaction ID, tool version, arguments digest, result digest, and timestamps.
misconceptions: Tool name alone correlates a result when calls are parallel or repeated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve provider call ID, local attempt ID, interaction ID, tool version, arguments digest, result digest, and timestamps.

competencies.statement: Validate and normalize model arguments before policy and execution while retaining rejected input evidence safely.
misconceptions: Provider schema adherence removes the need for application validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate and normalize model arguments before policy and execution while retaining rejected input evidence safely.

competencies.statement: Return a bounded typed success or failure envelope with safe user detail, retryability, effect identity, redaction, and provenance.
misconceptions: Returning exception text directly gives the model the best recovery context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return a bounded typed success or failure envelope with safe user detail, retryability, effect identity, redaction, and provenance.

competencies.statement: Reject unknown, disabled, stale, unauthorized, and version-mismatched tools without dynamic resolution or side effects.
misconceptions: A model-selected tool should be invoked if any similarly named function exists.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject unknown, disabled, stale, unauthorized, and version-mismatched tools without dynamic resolution or side effects.

competencies.statement: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
misconceptions: The agent may act with every permission held by the application service account.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.

competencies.statement: Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
misconceptions: Tool descriptions are an adequate security boundary for excessive agency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.

competencies.statement: Classify pure reads, sensitive reads, reversible writes, irreversible writes, external communication, financial, identity, and security effects.
misconceptions: All function calls have equivalent risk if their schemas validate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify pure reads, sensitive reads, reversible writes, irreversible writes, external communication, financial, identity, and security effects.

competencies.statement: Generate deterministic application-owned previews and diffs before effects without trusting model-written summaries of the action.
misconceptions: A natural-language confirmation generated by the model is a trustworthy action preview.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate deterministic application-owned previews and diffs before effects without trusting model-written summaries of the action.

competencies.statement: Deny by default, escalate ambiguous authority, revoke capabilities, stop in-flight work, rotate exposure, and audit policy changes.
misconceptions: Once an agent session starts, its tool authority must remain fixed until completion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Deny by default, escalate ambiguous authority, revoke capabilities, stop in-flight work, rotate exposure, and audit policy changes.

competencies.statement: Classify model output, function call, function result, built-in tool, retrieval, safety, error, and terminal interaction steps exhaustively.
misconceptions: Every successful interaction contains only one output_text value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify model output, function call, function result, built-in tool, retrieval, safety, error, and terminal interaction steps exhaustively.

competencies.statement: Reserve call identity before execution and reconcile retries so one logical function call cannot create duplicate effects.
misconceptions: A provider will never repeat a function call after a timeout.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reserve call identity before execution and reconcile retries so one logical function call cannot create duplicate effects.

competencies.statement: Return the exact function name, call ID, bounded typed result parts, and required tools and configuration in the follow-up interaction.
misconceptions: Sending raw result text in a new chat is equivalent to a correlated function result.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return the exact function name, call ID, bounded typed result parts, and required tools and configuration in the follow-up interaction.

competencies.statement: Preserve opaque provider state and required interaction history without interpreting hidden reasoning or dropping tool context.
misconceptions: Provider metadata can be reconstructed safely from visible text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve opaque provider state and required interaction history without interpreting hidden reasoning or dropping tool context.

competencies.statement: Link final output claims to interaction, call, arguments, result, model, schema, policy, and validation evidence.
misconceptions: A final answer is grounded whenever a tool was called somewhere in the conversation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Link final output claims to interaction, call, arguments, result, model, schema, policy, and validation evidence.

competencies.statement: Represent observe, request, classify, authorize, approve, execute, record, continue, complete, refuse, fail, cancel, and escalate transitions explicitly.
misconceptions: An agent loop needs only call model, call tool, and repeat.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent observe, request, classify, authorize, approve, execute, record, continue, complete, refuse, fail, cancel, and escalate transitions explicitly.

competencies.statement: Enforce steps, wall time, tokens, model calls, tool calls, retries, bytes, concurrency, and spend before each transition.
misconceptions: A maximum iteration count alone bounds every agent resource.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce steps, wall time, tokens, model calls, tool calls, retries, bytes, concurrency, and spend before each transition.

competencies.statement: Define observable state change and detect repeated calls, unchanged errors, oscillation, cyclic plans, and declining evidence quality.
misconceptions: Any new model message proves progress.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define observable state change and detect repeated calls, unchanged errors, oscillation, cyclic plans, and declining evidence quality.

competencies.statement: Emit exactly one typed complete, refused, exhausted, failed, cancelled, expired, or escalated terminal reason with partial evidence.
misconceptions: Stopping the Python function implies the agent completed successfully.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Emit exactly one typed complete, refused, exhausted, failed, cancelled, expired, or escalated terminal reason with partial evidence.

competencies.statement: Propagate cancellation through provider, tool, approval, task, stream, and checkpoint boundaries and reconcile uncertain effects.
misconceptions: Cancelling an asyncio task guarantees external work did not happen.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate cancellation through provider, tool, approval, task, stream, and checkpoint boundaries and reconcile uncertain effects.

competencies.statement: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.
misconceptions: Multiple calls returned together by a model are necessarily independent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.

competencies.statement: Own concurrent calls with asyncio TaskGroup, explicit timeout and semaphore budgets, and structured error and cancellation propagation.
misconceptions: Creating background tasks and retaining their references is equivalent to structured concurrency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Own concurrent calls with asyncio TaskGroup, explicit timeout and semaphore budgets, and structured error and cancellation propagation.

competencies.statement: Correlate each result by call ID and return required result parts in provider-valid order independent of completion order.
misconceptions: Appending results as tasks finish always preserves model call identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate each result by call ID and return required result parts in provider-valid order independent of completion order.

competencies.statement: Serialize or transactionally coordinate calls that touch shared resources, quotas, sessions, files, records, or external effects.
misconceptions: asyncio single-threaded execution prevents every shared-state race.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Serialize or transactionally coordinate calls that touch shared resources, quotas, sessions, files, records, or external effects.

competencies.statement: Cancel siblings when policy requires, collect partial outcomes, close handlers, reconcile uncertain effects, and prove no task survives the turn.
misconceptions: TaskGroup exit makes cleanup and external-effect reconciliation automatic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cancel siblings when policy requires, collect partial outcomes, close handlers, reconcile uncertain effects, and prove no task survives the turn.

competencies.statement: Trigger approval from effect class, value, uncertainty, novelty, policy, affected party, data sensitivity, and user settings before execution.
misconceptions: Approval is needed only when the model expresses uncertainty.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trigger approval from effect class, value, uncertainty, novelty, policy, affected party, data sensitivity, and user settings before execution.

competencies.statement: Render application-owned exact tool, arguments, target, before and after state, consequences, reversibility, cost, and alternatives accessibly.
misconceptions: A model-generated natural-language summary is sufficient for informed consent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Render application-owned exact tool, arguments, target, before and after state, consequences, reversibility, cost, and alternatives accessibly.

competencies.statement: Bind approval to user, session, policy, tool version, normalized arguments digest, preview digest, expiry, and one execution nonce.
misconceptions: Approval for a tool name authorizes any later arguments to that tool.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind approval to user, session, policy, tool version, normalized arguments digest, preview digest, expiry, and one execution nonce.

competencies.statement: Persist pending action and evidence atomically without holding open clients, locks, credentials, or unbounded provider state.
misconceptions: A paused coroutine is a durable approval workflow.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Persist pending action and evidence atomically without holding open clients, locks, credentials, or unbounded provider state.

competencies.statement: Resume only after revalidation; handle approve, reject, edit, revoke, expire, duplicate submission, changed policy, and already-completed action.
misconceptions: A stored yes remains valid regardless of elapsed time or changed state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resume only after revalidation; handle approve, reject, edit, revoke, expire, duplicate submission, changed policy, and already-completed action.

competencies.statement: Define tenant, user, session, invocation, interaction, turn, attempt, call, effect, checkpoint, and parent identities and their lifetimes.
misconceptions: One conversation ID can safely identify every layer of agent work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define tenant, user, session, invocation, interaction, turn, attempt, call, effect, checkpoint, and parent identities and their lifetimes.

competencies.statement: Record typed immutable state transitions with sequence, schema, causation, correlation, redaction, and integrity evidence.
misconceptions: Overwriting the latest session JSON is easier and equally auditable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record typed immutable state transitions with sequence, schema, causation, correlation, redaction, and integrity evidence.

competencies.statement: Commit state, pending calls, completed effects, budgets, approvals, and provider references atomically with version checks.
misconceptions: Saving messages is enough to resume an agent safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Commit state, pending calls, completed effects, budgets, approvals, and provider references atomically with version checks.

competencies.statement: Reconcile in-progress and uncertain effects before continuation using idempotency keys, receipts, status queries, or human review.
misconceptions: Restarting from the last model message cannot duplicate tool effects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile in-progress and uncertain effects before continuation using idempotency keys, receipts, status queries, or human review.

competencies.statement: Fork historical state into a new identity and replay with fakes or no-effect tools for debugging, evals, migration, and counterfactual comparison.
misconceptions: Replaying a production trajectory should re-execute its original tools for realism.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fork historical state into a new identity and replay with fakes or no-effect tools for debugging, evals, migration, and counterfactual comparison.

competencies.statement: Label each context item by source, authority, tenant, purpose, timestamp, sensitivity, trust, instruction status, and expiry.
misconceptions: All text in the context window has equal authority once tokenized.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Label each context item by source, authority, tenant, purpose, timestamp, sensitivity, trust, instruction status, and expiry.

competencies.statement: Reserve output and tool budget, measure or conservatively estimate input tokens, and admit context by task-specific value and risk.
misconceptions: Fitting under the model context limit prevents truncation and cost defects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reserve output and tool budget, measure or conservatively estimate input tokens, and admit context by task-specific value and risk.

competencies.statement: Preserve active policy, user goal, approvals, tool schemas, recent results, unresolved errors, and evidence while removing redundant or expired material.
misconceptions: Oldest-first deletion is a safe universal context policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve active policy, user goal, approvals, tool schemas, recent results, unresolved errors, and evidence while removing redundant or expired material.

competencies.statement: Create structured summaries with source references, retained decisions, rejected alternatives, open questions, limits, and uncertainty without hidden-reasoning claims.
misconceptions: A fluent summary preserves every operationally important fact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create structured summaries with source references, retained decisions, rejected alternatives, open questions, limits, and uncertainty without hidden-reasoning claims.

competencies.statement: Test pre- and post-compaction decisions on changed goals, permissions, failures, tool calls, citations, and adversarial instructions.
misconceptions: Lower token count is sufficient proof that compaction improved the agent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test pre- and post-compaction decisions on changed goals, permissions, failures, tool calls, citations, and adversarial instructions.

competencies.statement: Distinguish in-turn state, session memory, user preferences, task facts, organizational knowledge, examples, and audit records by owner and lifetime.
misconceptions: Agent memory is one vector store containing every prior message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish in-turn state, session memory, user preferences, task facts, organizational knowledge, examples, and audit records by owner and lifetime.

competencies.statement: Admit memory only for an allowed purpose with source, claim type, confidence, sensitivity, consent or authority, expiry, and contradiction policy.
misconceptions: The model should decide by itself which user statements become durable memory.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Admit memory only for an allowed purpose with source, claim type, confidence, sensitivity, consent or authority, expiry, and contradiction policy.

competencies.statement: Retrieve candidate memories with provenance and policy, then validate relevance, freshness, authority, tenant, and conflicts before context use.
misconceptions: High similarity means a memory is current, authorized, and true.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retrieve candidate memories with provenance and policy, then validate relevance, freshness, authority, tenant, and conflicts before context use.

competencies.statement: Enforce tenant and user isolation at storage, index, query, cache, logs, deletion, export, backup, and restore boundaries.
misconceptions: Adding a tenant filter to the final result prevents cross-tenant memory leaks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce tenant and user isolation at storage, index, query, cache, logs, deletion, export, backup, and restore boundaries.

competencies.statement: Test direct, indirect, cross-session, shared-memory, stale-fact, hidden-instruction, and authority-confusion poisoning with quarantine and repair paths.
misconceptions: Prompt instructions can reliably tell the model to ignore poisoned memories.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test direct, indirect, cross-session, shared-memory, stale-fact, hidden-instruction, and authority-confusion poisoning with quarantine and repair paths.

competencies.statement: Classify validation, safety, authentication, authorization, quota, rate, transient provider, terminal provider, tool, policy, timeout, cancellation, and uncertain-effect failures.
misconceptions: All provider errors are transient HTTP failures.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify validation, safety, authentication, authorization, quota, rate, transient provider, terminal provider, tool, policy, timeout, cancellation, and uncertain-effect failures.

competencies.statement: Retry only idempotent or protected operations when policy, deadline, budget, status, provider guidance, and attempt evidence permit.
misconceptions: Exponential backoff makes any failed operation safe to retry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retry only idempotent or protected operations when policy, deadline, budget, status, provider guidance, and attempt evidence permit.

competencies.statement: Apply bounded delay, Retry-After, jitter, global and per-resource concurrency, rate, token, and spend budgets without retry storms.
misconceptions: Independent callers with jitter cannot amplify load together.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply bounded delay, Retry-After, jitter, global and per-resource concurrency, rate, token, and spend budgets without retry storms.

competencies.statement: Use operation keys, effect receipts, status reconciliation, deduplication windows, and atomic state to prevent duplicate writes.
misconceptions: A request timeout proves the external action did not occur.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use operation keys, effect receipts, status reconciliation, deduplication windows, and atomic state to prevent duplicate writes.

competencies.statement: Design and test compensation where possible, preserve partial evidence, stop visibly when safety is uncertain, and escalate irreversible outcomes.
misconceptions: Every external effect can be rolled back automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design and test compensation where possible, preserve partial evidence, stop visibly when safety is uncertain, and escalate irreversible outcomes.

competencies.statement: Define typed lifecycle, model delta, tool request, approval, tool progress, result, warning, error, terminal, and usage events with stable identity.
misconceptions: An agent UI needs only assistant text tokens and a loading spinner.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define typed lifecycle, model delta, tool request, approval, tool progress, result, warning, error, terminal, and usage events with stable identity.

competencies.statement: Assemble ordered deltas and state snapshots idempotently while preserving call, interaction, retry, and terminal boundaries.
misconceptions: Appending every received event always reconstructs correct state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assemble ordered deltas and state snapshots idempotently while preserving call, interaction, retry, and terminal boundaries.

competencies.statement: Announce meaningful milestones through restrained live regions, expose structured text, keep headings and focus stable, and avoid color-only meaning.
misconceptions: Screen readers should announce every generated token for immediacy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Announce meaningful milestones through restrained live regions, expose structured text, keep headings and focus stable, and avoid color-only meaning.

competencies.statement: Provide keyboard-operable pause, cancel, approve, reject, retry, details, and recovery controls with visible focus and large targets.
misconceptions: A keyboard-accessible text box makes the whole agent workflow accessible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide keyboard-operable pause, cancel, approve, reject, retry, details, and recovery controls with visible focus and large targets.

competencies.statement: Resume streams from sequence or snapshot, deduplicate events, reconcile terminal state, and avoid replaying side effects after reconnect.
misconceptions: Opening a new stream after disconnect can safely start the agent turn again.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resume streams from sequence or snapshot, deduplicate events, reconcile terminal state, and avoid replaying side effects after reconnect.

competencies.statement: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.
misconceptions: Delimiters alone prevent untrusted data from becoming instructions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.

competencies.statement: Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.
misconceptions: Blocking phrases such as ignore previous instructions stops prompt injection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.

competencies.statement: Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
misconceptions: Output from an allowlisted tool is automatically trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.

competencies.statement: Constrain destination, data class, field flow, tool combinations, external communication, and covert channels with policy and audit evidence.
misconceptions: Least-privilege tools alone prevent sensitive data exfiltration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain destination, data class, field flow, tool combinations, external communication, and covert channels with policy and audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Encode or sanitize model and tool output by HTML, Markdown, URL, spreadsheet, shell, log, and document context and preserve accessible alternatives.
misconceptions: Escaping angle brackets makes model output safe in every destination.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode or sanitize model and tool output by HTML, Markdown, URL, spreadsheet, shell, log, and document context and preserve accessible alternatives.

competencies.statement: Inject secrets through approved runtime stores, scope and rotate them, prevent prompt or tool exposure, redact derivatives, and destroy test credentials.
misconceptions: Environment variables are always safe from model, logs, child processes, and crash reports.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject secrets through approved runtime stores, scope and rotate them, prevent prompt or tool exposure, redact derivatives, and destroy test credentials.

competencies.statement: Map purpose, legal or organizational authority, fields, sensitivity, users, provider transfer, storage, access, retention, deletion, and aggregation risk.
misconceptions: A useful model should receive every available field for context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map purpose, legal or organizational authority, fields, sensitivity, users, provider transfer, storage, access, retention, deletion, and aggregation risk.

competencies.statement: Record current paid, unpaid, logging, sharing, abuse-monitoring, region, age, and sensitive-use terms and re-review them before release.
misconceptions: One Gemini data policy applies to every service tier, region, and feature.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record current paid, unpaid, logging, sharing, abuse-monitoring, region, age, and sensitive-use terms and re-review them before release.

competencies.statement: Implement purpose-limited retention, user and administrator deletion, cache and provider-state expiry, backup handling, legal holds, and proof of deletion limits.
misconceptions: Deleting the local session deletes every provider log, cache, memory, and backup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement purpose-limited retention, user and administrator deletion, cache and provider-state expiry, backup handling, legal holds, and proof of deletion limits.

competencies.statement: Route medical, legal, employment, education, finance, identity, safety, minors, surveillance, and other high-impact cases to qualified policy review.
misconceptions: A disclaimer turns a high-impact agent into a low-risk application.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Route medical, legal, employment, education, finance, identity, safety, minors, surveillance, and other high-impact cases to qualified policy review.

competencies.statement: Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.
misconceptions: A few creative prompts represent real agent usage adequately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.

competencies.statement: Define exact schema, state, tool, argument, authorization, effect, citation, budget, stop, and recovery assertions wherever outcomes are deterministic.
misconceptions: Agent evaluation must use only model judges because responses are probabilistic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define exact schema, state, tool, argument, authorization, effect, citation, budget, stop, and recovery assertions wherever outcomes are deterministic.

competencies.statement: Create changed-input, changed-policy, tool-fault, injection, memory-poisoning, approval, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion cases without leaking answers.
misconceptions: One jailbreak list permanently covers agent security regressions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create changed-input, changed-policy, tool-fault, injection, memory-poisoning, approval, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion cases without leaking answers.

competencies.statement: Record deterministic workflow, prior prompt, prior model, no-tool, human, and current production baselines with confidence and cost.
misconceptions: A new model should be compared only with its own previous run.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record deterministic workflow, prior prompt, prior model, no-tool, human, and current production baselines with confidence and cost.

competencies.statement: Version dataset, split, rubric, oracle, tool fixtures, model, SDK, policy, seed where supported, runner, and result artifacts.
misconceptions: An eval score is reproducible without versioning its cases and environment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version dataset, split, rubric, oracle, tool fixtures, model, SDK, policy, seed where supported, runner, and result artifacts.

competencies.statement: Capture observable state, interaction steps, tool calls and results, approvals, effects, retries, budgets, errors, and terminal reason without hidden-reasoning claims.
misconceptions: Final text contains enough evidence to reconstruct how an agent acted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture observable state, interaction steps, tool calls and results, approvals, effects, retries, budgets, errors, and terminal reason without hidden-reasoning claims.

competencies.statement: Choose exact, in-order, any-order, partial-order, forbidden-step, or state-invariant matching based on task semantics.
misconceptions: Every valid agent trajectory must exactly match one reference sequence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose exact, in-order, any-order, partial-order, forbidden-step, or state-invariant matching based on task semantics.

competencies.statement: Measure required-tool recall, tool precision, unnecessary calls, forbidden calls, schema validity, argument correctness, and result use.
misconceptions: Tool-call count is a sufficient measure of agent efficiency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure required-tool recall, tool precision, unnecessary calls, forbidden calls, schema validity, argument correctness, and result use.

competencies.statement: Verify actual state changes, idempotency, approvals, receipts, cleanup, compensation, and affected-user outcomes rather than call syntax alone.
misconceptions: A correct tool name and arguments prove the external effect succeeded.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify actual state changes, idempotency, approvals, receipts, cleanup, compensation, and affected-user outcomes rather than call syntax alone.

competencies.statement: Assess complete, refuse, escalate, exhausted, cancelled, failed, partial, rollback, and recovery outcomes under injected faults.
misconceptions: Only successful completion cases belong in an agent evaluation set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assess complete, refuse, escalate, exhausted, cancelled, failed, partial, rollback, and recovery outcomes under injected faults.

competencies.statement: Define observable task-specific criteria, anchors, critical failures, accessibility, safety, evidence, and scoring aggregation before viewing candidate outputs.
misconceptions: A generic helpfulness rubric transfers unchanged to every agent task.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define observable task-specific criteria, anchors, critical failures, accessibility, safety, evidence, and scoring aggregation before viewing candidate outputs.

competencies.statement: Blind model, prompt, provider, and treatment identity; randomize order; use pairwise comparison when it improves judgment reliability.
misconceptions: Judge models are immune to model-name, order, verbosity, and style bias.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Blind model, prompt, provider, and treatment identity; randomize order; use pairwise comparison when it improves judgment reliability.

competencies.statement: Measure repeatability, position effects, rubric sensitivity, reference leakage, adversarial manipulation, and judge-version drift.
misconceptions: A deterministic temperature makes a model judge objectively correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure repeatability, position effects, rubric sensitivity, reference leakage, adversarial manipulation, and judge-version drift.

competencies.statement: Use trained reviewers, independent labels, adjudication, disagreement records, affected-domain expertise, and sampled production calibration.
misconceptions: Human review needs no rubric or reliability measurement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use trained reviewers, independent labels, adjudication, disagreement records, affected-domain expertise, and sampled production calibration.

competencies.statement: Give deterministic safety and policy failures precedence, retain metric disagreement, report uncertainty, and escalate high-impact ambiguous cases.
misconceptions: A high average judge score can compensate for a failed authorization invariant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give deterministic safety and policy failures precedence, retain metric disagreement, report uncertainty, and escalate high-impact ambiguous cases.

competencies.statement: Correlate service request, session, invocation, interaction, model call, tool call, approval, retry, effect, and recovery spans and events.
misconceptions: One span around the final response is sufficient agent observability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate service request, session, invocation, interaction, model call, tool call, approval, retry, effect, and recovery spans and events.

competencies.statement: Apply versioned OpenTelemetry GenAI operation, provider, model, usage, tool, workflow, error, and output attributes with stability awareness.
misconceptions: Experimental GenAI semantic conventions can be treated as stable forever.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply versioned OpenTelemetry GenAI operation, provider, model, usage, tool, workflow, error, and output attributes with stability awareness.

competencies.statement: Default to metadata and hashes; sample, minimize, redact, encrypt, access-control, retain, and delete prompt, result, and tool content only when approved.
misconceptions: Full prompt and response logging is required to debug agents.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Default to metadata and hashes; sample, minimize, redact, encrypt, access-control, retain, and delete prompt, result, and tool content only when approved.

competencies.statement: Reconcile input, cached, output, reasoning where exposed, tool, retry, and total token usage to model and pricing version without hard-coded permanence.
misconceptions: Character counts accurately represent provider token billing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile input, cached, output, reasoning where exposed, tool, retry, and total token usage to model and pricing version without hard-coded permanence.

competencies.statement: Reconstruct first failing policy, context, model, tool, approval, effect, retry, or resource boundary and distinguish cause from downstream symptoms.
misconceptions: The final exception is usually the root cause of an agent incident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconstruct first failing policy, context, model, tool, approval, effect, retry, or resource boundary and distinguish cause from downstream symptoms.

competencies.statement: Measure queue, provider time to first event, generation, tool, approval, retry, serialization, and end-to-end latency by outcome and percentile.
misconceptions: Average model response latency predicts user-visible agent latency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure queue, provider time to first event, generation, tool, approval, retry, serialization, and end-to-end latency by outcome and percentile.

competencies.statement: Bound sessions, provider calls, tool calls, per-tenant work, queues, task groups, connection pools, and downstream effects with fair admission.
misconceptions: More concurrent model calls always reduce task completion time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound sessions, provider calls, tool calls, per-tenant work, queues, task groups, connection pools, and downstream effects with fair admission.

competencies.statement: Enforce project and tenant requests, tokens, daily and rolling spend, retry, model, and tool budgets before work starts and while it runs.
misconceptions: Provider rate limits alone prevent denial-of-wallet incidents.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce project and tenant requests, tokens, daily and rolling spend, retry, model, and tool budgets before work starts and while it runs.

competencies.statement: Use implicit or explicit context caching only with correct prefix identity, tenant isolation, expiry, invalidation, retention, privacy, and measured reuse value.
misconceptions: Cached context is free, current, and outside retention concerns.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use implicit or explicit context caching only with correct prefix identity, tenant isolation, expiry, invalidation, retention, privacy, and measured reuse value.

competencies.statement: Run authorized fixed-distribution load, saturation, cancellation, quota, spend, dependency, and recovery experiments and state environment limits.
misconceptions: A mocked provider benchmark proves production capacity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run authorized fixed-distribution load, saturation, cancellation, quota, spend, dependency, and recovery experiments and state environment limits.

competencies.statement: Map product policy, provider port, loop, tool registry, session, memory, approval, events, evals, and telemetry to ADK concepts and identify gaps.
misconceptions: Using ADK automatically preserves every custom core invariant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map product policy, provider port, loop, tool registry, session, memory, approval, events, evals, and telemetry to ADK concepts and identify gaps.

competencies.statement: Configure Agent, model, instruction, tools, Runner, invocation, events, and async lifecycle with explicit versions and bounded behavior.
misconceptions: Creating an ADK Agent object is equivalent to a production agent service.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure Agent, model, instruction, tools, Runner, invocation, events, and async lifecycle with explicit versions and bounded behavior.

competencies.statement: Select and test ADK session and memory services for identity, persistence, isolation, migration, retention, and replay boundaries.
misconceptions: An ADK memory service makes every stored item safe and relevant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select and test ADK session and memory services for identity, persistence, isolation, migration, retention, and replay boundaries.

competencies.statement: Use callbacks and plugins for observable bounded cross-cutting policy without hidden mutation, ordering assumptions, or conflicting decisions.
misconceptions: More callbacks always improve agent control and observability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use callbacks and plugins for observable bounded cross-cutting policy without hidden mutation, ordering assumptions, or conflicting decisions.

competencies.statement: Verify tool confirmation, policy, evaluation, event, error, cancellation, and deployment behavior against framework-independent conformance cases.
misconceptions: A framework confirmation dialog is sufficient proof of informed approval.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify tool confirmation, policy, evaluation, event, error, cancellation, and deployment behavior against framework-independent conformance cases.

competencies.statement: Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.
misconceptions: More agents improve difficult tasks through extra reasoning automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.

competencies.statement: Define delegated goal, inputs, outputs, non-goals, authority, tools, budget, deadline, evidence, stop, and escalation in a typed contract.
misconceptions: A natural-language role name adequately constrains a subagent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define delegated goal, inputs, outputs, non-goals, authority, tools, budget, deadline, evidence, stop, and escalation in a typed contract.

competencies.statement: Send only task-required, tenant-authorized, provenance-labeled context and never inherit parent secrets or tools implicitly.
misconceptions: Subagents need the full parent transcript to avoid mistakes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Send only task-required, tenant-authorized, provenance-labeled context and never inherit parent secrets or tools implicitly.

competencies.statement: Validate independent results, preserve provenance and disagreement, detect conflicts, rerun changed cases, and assign merge authority.
misconceptions: The coordinator can safely summarize conflicting agent outputs without verification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate independent results, preserve provenance and disagreement, detect conflicts, rerun changed cases, and assign merge authority.

competencies.statement: Bound fan-out, depth, shared memory, tool authority, retries, spend, cascading cancellation, poisoned results, and partial recovery.
misconceptions: Independent agent processes prevent logical cascading failures.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound fan-out, depth, shared memory, tool authority, retries, spend, cascading cancellation, poisoned results, and partial recovery.

competencies.statement: Classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries.
misconceptions: Python syntax validation makes generated code safe to execute.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries.

competencies.statement: Run code in a disposable isolated environment with deny-by-default capabilities, immutable base, bounded resources, no host mounts, and complete destruction.
misconceptions: A virtual environment isolates generated code from host files and network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run code in a disposable isolated environment with deny-by-default capabilities, immutable base, bounded resources, no host mounts, and complete destruction.

competencies.statement: Mediate files, environment, network destinations, DNS, uploads, downloads, subprocesses, and credentials through typed policy and explicit approvals.
misconceptions: Blocking shell commands prevents generated code from accessing external systems.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Mediate files, environment, network destinations, DNS, uploads, downloads, subprocesses, and credentials through typed policy and explicit approvals.

competencies.statement: Treat screen, page, document, email, and accessibility-tree content as untrusted; constrain targets and actions; preview sensitive effects; resist dialog forging.
misconceptions: A visual browser surface is safer than API tools because users can see it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat screen, page, document, email, and accessibility-tree content as untrusted; constrain targets and actions; preview sensitive effects; resist dialog forging.

competencies.statement: Validate type, size, structure, malware risk, secrets, active content, accessibility, provenance, and destination before retaining or publishing artifacts.
misconceptions: An artifact created successfully inside a sandbox is safe to distribute.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate type, size, structure, malware risk, secrets, active content, accessibility, provenance, and destination before retaining or publishing artifacts.

competencies.statement: Separate domain policy, provider adapter, orchestration, tools, persistence, interfaces, and composition root in an import-safe typed package.
misconceptions: Putting the loop in one main.py file reduces production complexity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate domain policy, provider adapter, orchestration, tools, persistence, interfaces, and composition root in an import-safe typed package.

competencies.statement: Expose consistent request, event, terminal, error, cancellation, idempotency, and version behavior through accessible CLI and authenticated API surfaces.
misconceptions: A CLI print loop and HTTP endpoint can use unrelated outcome semantics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose consistent request, event, terminal, error, cancellation, idempotency, and version behavior through accessible CLI and authenticated API surfaces.

competencies.statement: Validate environment-specific non-secret configuration, inject secret references separately, reject unsafe defaults, and publish a redacted effective config.
misconceptions: Environment variables need no schema or startup validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate environment-specific non-secret configuration, inject secret references separately, reject unsafe defaults, and publish a redacted effective config.

competencies.statement: Create and close provider, tool, persistence, telemetry, and task resources in explicit lifespan; distinguish liveness, readiness, and dependency degradation.
misconceptions: A process listening on a port is ready for safe agent work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create and close provider, tool, persistence, telemetry, and task resources in explicit lifespan; distinguish liveness, readiness, and dependency degradation.

competencies.statement: Bind revision, package, dependency lock, policy, model config, eval set, artifact digest, environment, migration, and rollback identity.
misconceptions: A deployment timestamp is sufficient agent release identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind revision, package, dependency lock, policy, model config, eval set, artifact digest, environment, migration, and rollback identity.

competencies.statement: Require schema, unit, trajectory, safety, privacy, accessibility, compatibility, load, cost, deployment, migration, rollback, recovery, and review evidence before release.
misconceptions: A passing end-to-end demo is a sufficient agent release gate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require schema, unit, trajectory, safety, privacy, accessibility, compatibility, load, cost, deployment, migration, rollback, recovery, and review evidence before release.

competencies.statement: Diff model, API, SDK, ADK, schema, safety, tool, token, state, and deprecation behavior against versioned fixtures and representative evals.
misconceptions: Changing only a model identifier is a low-risk configuration update.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diff model, API, SDK, ADK, schema, safety, tool, token, state, and deprecation behavior against versioned fixtures and representative evals.

competencies.statement: Canary immutable candidates against task, safety, tool, refusal, latency, cost, and user signals with predeclared abort and state-aware rollback.
misconceptions: A canary is safe whenever its average response score improves.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Canary immutable candidates against task, safety, tool, refusal, latency, cost, and user signals with predeclared abort and state-aware rollback.

competencies.statement: Stop new work, cancel safely, revoke tools and credentials, preserve evidence, reconcile effects, restore state, notify owners, and rehearse recovery.
misconceptions: Disabling model calls is enough to contain every agent incident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stop new work, cancel safely, revoke tools and credentials, preserve evidence, reconcile effects, restore state, notify owners, and rehearse recovery.

competencies.statement: Defend stakeholder value, affected-user protection, evidence quality, limitations, support, monitoring, re-review, ownership, and accepted residual risk.
misconceptions: Passing technical checks transfers responsibility from the release team to the model provider.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend stakeholder value, affected-user protection, evidence quality, limitations, support, monitoring, re-review, ownership, and accepted residual risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Safe Evaluated AI Agent with Python 3.14 and Gemini 3.5 Certificate
